rulings.Case Title: TRACEY THOMPSON VS. JOHN STEWART CO. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S Motion To Strike GRANTED. Paragraphs 666, 71, 107, 110 and Prayer for Relief Nos. 3 and 7 are stricken in so far as they allege punitive damages against SFHA, a government entity. (See Gov. Code §818.) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: TRACEY THOMPSON VS. JOHN STEWART CO. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 1. DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED with leave to amend as follows: Allege facts sufficient to demonstrate compliance with administrative exhaustion. Allege facts sufficient to demonstrate compliance with Govt. Code 911.2 If Plaintiff wishes to bring all causes of action against all defendants, Plaintiff must allege sufficient facts to sustain each cause of action against each defendant, including SFHA. Alternatively, if Defendant would like to request a continuance of a hearing to allow sufficient time to file an addition Reply, they may do so. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GLORIA YEE VS. FAT NOODLE 2ND SF, LLC, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion By Lee Weinberg And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Notice Of Lodgment Of Exhibits 1 Through 21; Declarations Of Lee Weinberg And Ronald F. Berestka
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 7. INTERVENOR LEE WEINBERG, UNITED STATES LIABILITY INSURANCE COMPANY MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion By Lee Weinberg And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, Summary Adjudication; Statement Of Undisputed Material Facts; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support; Notice Of Lodgment Of Exhibits 1 Through 21; Declarations Of Lee Weinberg And Ronald F. Berestka Motion for Summary Judgment is also DENIED. Motion for Summary Adjudication is DENIED. The Court finds triable issues of material fact as to both negligence theories asserted against Defendant. Defendant, in fact, seemingly concedes the existence of many factual disputes regarding material issues. (See Weinberg's Response to Plaintiff's Separate Statement of Undisputed Facts, pp. 4-13.) Issues include: " Whether Defendant made any personal representations or guarantees regarding the pertinent lease, and if so, to what extent. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 2, p.95:8-25; Ex.3, pp.67:21-68:7; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Weinberg Decl. 6.) " Whether, and to what extent, did Defendant assume direction and responsibility of the renovation project, or any portion thereof. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 4, p.87:10-18; Ex. 7; Ex. 9; Ex. 10; Ex. 11; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Weinberg Decl. 9-10.) " Whether Defendant authorized any unpermitted renovation work, and if so, whether Defendant acted as a key decision maker. (Plaintiff's NOL, Ex. 5, 79-80, 83:1-16, 147:15-25; Ex. 7; Ex. 11; Patton Decl. 6-8; Defendant's NOL, Ex. 3, pp. 108:4-109:15, 137:4-14; Ex. 4, pp. 119:16-121:18; Weinberg Decl. 8-12.) = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GEORI TOLOZA ET AL VS. DENG BOUTDY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Ccp 473.5 Ccp 473d And Quash Service Of Summons Ccp 418.10
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 2. DEFENDANT DENG BOUTDY, MAY BOUTDY, MALAYKHAM BOUTDY Notice Of Motion And Motion To Set Aside Default Ccp 473.5 Ccp 473d And Quash Service Of Summons Ccp 418.10 DENIED in part. Hearing Required in part. To the extent Defendant is moving to set aside default based on CCP 473(b), the motion is DENIED. The argument is raised for the first time in reply. (See Notice of Motion). Hearing Required to address applicability of Cal. Civ. Code 1961 and CCP 473.5. All Defendants and Rene Boutdy to be present. = (501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: GOLDEN GATEWAY CENTER, SPE, LLC VS. P SAMUELSON ART ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for May 10, 2019 line 6. DEFENDANT ARTHUR SAMUELSON DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint states sufficient facts to assert an unlawful detainer cause of action and is not uncertain. = (501/CFH)
